_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 2-8 Drowning/Submersion Injury (Revised 05/23/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Trauma and Environmental Protocol Section 2-8: Drowning/Submersion Injury — MDHHS Approved 5/23/23, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: General Management
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 2-8: Drowning/Submersion Injury — General Management
points: Follow General Pre-Hospital Care-Treatment Protocol
ALL submersion patients should be transported for observation and evaluation regardless of apparent recovery at the scene
Remove the patient from the water as rapidly as possible while maintaining a supine position
Protect the cervical spine when the mechanism suggests spinal injury, such as diving, watercraft collision, or unknown mechanism
Patients in cardiac arrest from submersion: follow Adult or Pediatric General Cardiac Arrest-Treatment Protocol
If water temperature is uncertain, consider it cold water for resuscitation decision-making
Submersion time is counted from when the patient is presumed to have gone underwater

sections.title: Airway and Ventilation
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 2-8: Drowning/Submersion Injury — Airway and Ventilation
points: Administer high-flow oxygen and proactively manage airway, oxygenation, and ventilation
Ventilate as needed with BVM and oxygen
If patient remains hypoxic despite high-flow oxygen: consider CPAP per MCA selection; CPAP may be selected as a BLS skill by the MCA
Advanced airway if indicated per Airway Management-Procedure Protocol
Evaluate for associated injuries, head injury, diving emergency, and hyperbaric chamber considerations; contact Medical Control early when indicated

sections.title: Cold Water Submersion
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 2-8: Drowning/Submersion Injury — Cold Water
points: Cold water submersion is water temperature less than 70°F / 21°C
If presumed submersion time is less than 90 minutes, initiate resuscitative efforts
If presumed submersion time is greater than 90 minutes, refer to Dead on Scene/Termination of Resuscitation and contact Medical Control
Contact Medical Control for transport timing and destination considerations, including rewarming capability
Refer to Hypothermia/Frostbite-Treatment Protocol, prevent further heat loss, dry and wrap the patient in a vapor barrier, and handle gently

sections.title: Warm Water Submersion
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 2-8: Drowning/Submersion Injury — Warm Water
points: Warm water submersion is water temperature greater than or equal to 70°F / 21°C
If presumed submersion time is less than 30 minutes, initiate resuscitative efforts
If presumed submersion time is greater than 30 minutes, refer to Dead on Scene/Termination of Resuscitation and contact Medical Control
Contact Medical Control for transport timing, destination, or termination of resuscitation guidance

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must indicate whether CPAP is authorized for use in submersion patients with persistent hypoxia

deterioration_flags: Persistent hypoxia despite high-flow oxygen — consider CPAP (per MCA selection); advanced airway if available
Vomiting and airway compromise — suction immediately; reposition; consider advanced airway
Cardiac arrest — begin CPR immediately; follow appropriate arrest protocol; do not delay for water removal from airway
Cold water submersion less than 90 minutes — initiate resuscitative efforts and contact Medical Control for transport/destination guidance
Warm water submersion greater than 30 minutes or cold water submersion greater than 90 minutes — refer to Dead on Scene/Termination of Resuscitation and contact Medical Control
scope_notes: Scene safety, basic resuscitation, oxygen administration, BVM ventilation: all levels
CPAP: per MCA selection — may be BLS or Specialist
IV/IO access and fluid administration: Specialist skill
